item.title: Sky Gold & Diam. Joins Mojo Stocks with Strong Financial Momentum
item.description: Sky Gold & Diam., a small-cap player in the Gems, Jewellery and Watches sector, has been added to the prestigious Mojo Stocks list as of 28 April 2026, reflecting its robust financial performance and bullish momentum. The company’s recent quarterly results demonstrate significant growth across key metrics, positioning it as a compelling investment opportunity within the small-cap universe. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Skipper Joins Reliable Performers List Following Strong Q4 Financials
item.description: Skipper, a small-cap player in the heavy electrical equipment sector, has been added to the esteemed Reliable Performers list as of 28 April 2026, following a robust quarterly performance that signals sustained growth and operational strength. The stock’s recent financial metrics and improving fundamentals position it favourably for investors seeking consistent returns in a challenging market environment. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: Greenply Industr Joins Hidden Turnarounds List Following Strong Q4 Financials
item.description: Greenply Industr, a small-cap player in the plywood and laminates sector, has been added to the prestigious Hidden Turnarounds list as of 28 April 2026, following a remarkable quarter ending March 2026. This inclusion reflects the company’s significant financial turnaround and positions it as a compelling investment opportunity within the small-cap universe. Time: 1 hour ago

item.title: The Leela Palaces Q4 FY26: Luxury Hospitality Giant Posts Record Profits But Valuation Concerns Mount
item.description: Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts Ltd., India's only institutionally owned and managed pure-play luxury hospitality company, delivered its strongest quarterly performance on record in Q4 FY26, with consolidated net profit surging 46.17% year-on-year to ₹171.77 crores. However, the stock has declined 2.92% year-to-date, trading at ₹420.40 with a market capitalisation of ₹14,203 crores, as investors grapple with stretched valuations and concerns about the sustainability of the company's modest return on equity of just 1.34%. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Sapphire Foods Q4 FY26: Mounting Losses Deepen as Margins Compress Amid Expansion Costs
item.description: Sapphire Foods India Limited, the master franchisee for KFC and Pizza Hut in India, reported a consolidated net loss of ₹12.61 crores for Q4 FY26 ended March 2026, marking a steep deterioration from the ₹1.79 crores profit posted in the corresponding quarter last year. The loss widened by 163.26% sequentially from Q3 FY26's ₹4.79 crores loss, signalling persistent operational challenges despite revenue growth. The stock, currently trading at ₹174.80, has plunged 45.54% over the past year and remains 52.51% below its 52-week high of ₹368.10, reflecting investor concerns about profitability and margin sustainability. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Dalmia Bharat Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Underlying Concerns
item.description: Dalmia Bharat Ltd., one of India's leading cement manufacturers with a cumulative installed capacity of 44.60 million tonnes per annum, reported a consolidated net profit of ₹387.00 crores for Q4 FY26, marking a dramatic sequential surge of 217.21% from the previous quarter's ₹122.00 crores. However, the year-on-year comparison reveals a more sobering reality, with profits declining 11.03% from ₹435.00 crores in Q4 FY25. The stock, currently trading at ₹1,923.80 with a market capitalisation of ₹36,388.00 crores, declined 2.52% on April 28, 2026, as investors grappled with mixed signals from the results. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: LKP Securities Q4 FY26: Recovery Amid Volatility, But Structural Challenges Persist
item.description: LKP Securities Ltd., a Mumbai-based capital markets firm with a legacy spanning seven decades, reported a sequential recovery in Q4 FY26 with net profit surging 135.42% quarter-on-quarter to ₹2.26 crores. However, the year-on-year comparison reveals persistent headwinds, with net profit declining 15.99% from ₹2.69 crores in Q4 FY25. The stock rallied sharply, closing at ₹22.10 on April 28, 2026—a remarkable 10.50% gain in a single session—reflecting investor optimism about the sequential turnaround, though the company's ₹166 crore market capitalisation positions it firmly in micro-cap territory. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Maruti Suzuki Q4 FY26: Revenue Surge Masks Profit Decline in Volume-Driven Quarter
item.description: Maruti Suzuki India Ltd., the country's largest passenger vehicle manufacturer with a market capitalisation of ₹4,17,967 crores, reported a mixed performance in Q4 FY26, with consolidated net profit declining 6.45% year-on-year to ₹3,659.00 crores despite robust revenue growth. The quarter-on-quarter decline of 5.67% from ₹3,879.10 crores in Q3 FY26 raised concerns amongst investors, though the company posted its highest-ever quarterly revenue of ₹52,462.50 crores, marking a 28.21% surge compared to the year-ago period. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Dhanlaxmi Bank Q4 FY26: Strong Profit Surge Masks Underlying Asset Quality Concerns
item.description: Dhanlaxmi Bank Ltd., the Thrissur-headquartered private sector lender with a market capitalisation of ₹1,105.00 crores, delivered a robust quarterly performance in Q4 FY26, posting a net profit of ₹43.49 crores—a sequential surge of 82.12% from Q3 FY26's ₹23.88 crores and a year-on-year growth of 50.07% from ₹28.98 crores in Q4 FY25. The stock responded enthusiastically, surging 13.93% to ₹31.90 on April 28, 2026, as investors welcomed the headline numbers. However, beneath the impressive profit growth lies a more nuanced story of elevated provisions, declining asset quality metrics, and a capital adequacy ratio that requires close monitoring. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Artson Ltd Q4 FY25: Profitability Returns But Structural Challenges Persist
item.description: Artson Engineering Limited, a Mumbai-based project engineering company specialising in fuel storage and handling facility systems, reported a return to quarterly profitability in Q4 FY25 (March quarter) with net profit of ₹3.36 crores, reversing from a loss of ₹12.22 crores in the previous quarter. However, the micro-cap company with a market capitalisation of ₹572.00 crores continues to grapple with fundamental weaknesses that have plagued its performance over recent years. The stock, currently trading at ₹159.30, has gained 2.77% following the results but remains down 6.81% over the past year, significantly underperforming the industrial manufacturing sector's 28.41% return. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Mahindra Lifespace Q4 FY26: Strong Revenue Surge Masks Underlying Operational Concerns
item.description: Mahindra Lifespace Developers Ltd., one of India's leading real estate development companies and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Mahindra & Mahindra Ltd., reported a remarkable revenue surge in Q4 FY26, though the headline numbers conceal deeper operational challenges that warrant investor caution. The ₹6,982-crore market capitalisation company posted consolidated net profit of ₹90.11 crores for the quarter ended March 2026, representing a 5.91% year-on-year increase but a 17.24% sequential decline from the previous quarter. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Are Unicommerce eSolutions Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Unicommerce eSolutions Ltd's latest results show mixed performance, with a sequential decline in net sales and a significant drop in net profit, raising concerns about operational efficiency. While there is year-on-year revenue growth, the company needs to address its profitability challenges to regain investor confidence. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are Bajaj Housing Finance Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Bajaj Housing Finance Ltd's latest results show a slight net profit growth of 0.65% to ₹669.19 crores, with strong interest income growth of 15.69% year-on-year, but faced challenges from rising costs and competitive pressures, leading to a minor contraction in profit margins and operational efficiency. Overall, the performance reflects a balance between revenue growth and profitability amidst a challenging environment. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are Fidel Softech Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Fidel Softech Ltd's latest results are positive, showcasing a 155.10% year-on-year revenue growth and a net profit increase of 42.16%. However, the decline in operating margins due to rising employee costs requires careful monitoring moving forward. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are Rossari Biotech Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Rossari Biotech Ltd's latest results show strong revenue and profit growth, but concerns arise from declining operating margins and rising debt levels, indicating potential challenges ahead. Overall, while the figures are positive, operational issues may affect investor confidence. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are Adani Total Gas Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Adani Total Gas Ltd's latest results show strong revenue growth with a net profit of ₹168.34 crore, but profitability is under pressure due to margin compression and rising interest costs, indicating challenges in sustaining profits amidst competitive and regulatory pressures. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are Tamilnad Mercantile Bank Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Tamilnad Mercantile Bank Ltd's latest Q4 FY26 results are strong, with a net profit of ₹373.65 crores, a 28.01% year-on-year increase, and a gross NPA ratio of 0.73%. However, the high reliance on non-operating income (48.01% of profit before tax) raises concerns about earnings sustainability. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are SBI Cards & Payment Services Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: SBI Cards & Payment Services Ltd reported a net profit of ₹609.30 crores for Q1 2026, a 14.07% increase year-on-year, marking its highest quarterly earnings. However, revenue growth has moderated, raising concerns about sustainability and contributing to a 22.52% decline in stock performance over the past year. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are InfoBeans Technologies Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: InfoBeans Technologies Ltd's latest results show strong revenue growth of 37.40% and a net profit increase of 104.19%, marking a record quarter. However, operating margins have contracted significantly, raising concerns about sustainability amid rising employee costs. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are AVI Polymers Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: AVI Polymers Ltd's latest results show strong sequential growth, with net sales up 13.57% and net profit rising 46.08%. However, concerns about a drastic decrease in promoter holding and high valuation metrics suggest potential risks for investors. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: {{list.post_title}}
item.description: {{list.post_excerpt}} Time: {{list.post_date_sep}}
